授業目的: The objective of this course is to give students an overview of the key topics in international accounting and to introduce them to the international dimensions of financial statement preparationanalysis. The students will learn about differences in financial measurement and reporting practices that exist internationally, the reasons for these differences, their resultant financial statement effects and methods that they can employ to cope with such differences. The students will also learn about the international financial reporting standards (IFRS) movement and the implications of reading financial statements based on IFRS. As conceptual and practical applications of accounting are investigated from a global perspective, special emphasis is placed on managing multinational enterprises with respect to how accounting applies to global strategies and the key accounting issues that influence multinational decision making.
到達目標: Upon successful completion of the course, the students will be able to:•	Identify the relevant accounting issues confronted by business organizations that operate in the international arena and understand the development and need of international accounting. •	Explain the reasons for accounting diversity across counties and develop the links between accounting and the environment in which it operates.•	Explain the efforts towards the achievements of and obstacles for international accounting harmonization and assess the consequences of political lobbying on accounting standards.•	Apply international financial reporting standards in preparing group financial statements and interpret international financial statements for a multinational reporting entity.•	Describe how transfer pricing systems, in conjunction with taxation systems, can be used to achieve the corporate global objectives of multinational enterprises.
関連科目: Introduction to Financial Accounting, Management, Financial Reporting and Analysis
授業方法: This course will use a variety of teaching methods including lectures (including on-demand sessions), assigned readings, discussion and group assignments. On-demand materials are provided through my  Box link and LUNA course websites. (Click the below link)  XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX (Place BOX URL here)

第1回:
【Startup Week】
"Online class (On-demand format)" /《30minutes》
Watch Orientation movie before the 1st face-to-face session.
Review contents from the beginning.
第2回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class (On-demand format)" /《60minutes》
Discuss briefly each content of International Accounting course to give prospective students a comprehensive guidance on the syllabus. This video will enable the prospective students to understand how accounting is practiced by international organizations and reporting policies are shaped by economic, legal, social, cultural and technological factors. After watching this video, the potential students will be able to evaluate the importance of enrolling in this course in the rapidly changing global business environment.
第3回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, June 1
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Introduction to International Accounting
Historical development of accounting, The global environment of accounting, The nature and growth of MNCs, Cross-border mergers and acquisitions, Internationalization of capital markets.
Reading outside class: Choi and Meek: Chapter 1
第4回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, June 4
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International Accounting Differences: Causes and Examples
Causes of international differences in accounting: culture, legal systems, economic development, providers of finance, taxation system, status of accounting profession, and other external influences, Nature of differences in accounting standards and practices, National differences at the end of the twentieth century, How international differences impact on the application of IFRS standards.
Reading outside class: Nobe and Parker: Chapter 2, Alexander et al.: Chapter 2
第5回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, June 8
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Comparative Accounting: America, Europe and Asia 
Understanding and comparing five national financial accounting systems in Europe: France, Germany, Czech Republic, The Netherlands and United Kingdom, Understanding and comparing five national financial accounting systems in America and Asia: United States, Mexico, Japan, China and India.
Reading outside class: Choi and Meek: Chapter 3, 4
第6回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, June 11
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
From Harmonization to IFRS Standards as Globally Accepted Standards
International accounting harmonization and international accounting convergence, Reasons for, obstacles to and measurement of harmonization, Role of International Accounting Standards Committee/International Accounting Standards Board in accounting harmonization, IFRS Standards as Global Standards? Due process of setting accountng standards, Challenges for IFRS standards and the Board.
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 3, Nobe and Parker: Chapter 4
第7回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, June 15
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
The IASB Conceptual Framework and Political Lobbying on Accounting Standards
The role of accounting conceptual frameworks, The development of the IASB conceptual Framework, The 2018 IASB conceptual framework for financial reporting, Motivations for political lobbying, Political lobbying of the IASC/IASB, Political lobbying of the FASB’s convergence with the IASB.
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 4, Nobe and Parker: Chapter 11
第8回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, June 18
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International Accounting for Tangible and Intangible Assets 
IAS 16: Property, Plant and Equipment, IAS 23: Borrowing Cost, IAS 36: Impairment of Assets, IAS 38: Intangible Assets, IAS 40: Investment Property, IFRS 5: Non-Current Assets Held for Sale and Discontinued Operations, IFRS 16: Leases.
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 12, 13, 14, 15
第9回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class (On-demand format)" /《90minutes》
Discussion on accounting standard setting bodies around the world, the need of international accounting standards, due process of setting accounting standard and the economics and politics of accounting standard setting.
第10回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, June 22
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International Accounting for Liabilities, Provisions, Contingencies and Income Taxes 
IAS 37: Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets, IFRS 9: Financial Instruments,  IAS 12: Income Taxes.
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 17, 19, 20
第11回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, June 25
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International Accounting for Reporting Financial Performance 
IFRS 15: Revenue from Contracts with Customers, IAS 33: Earnings per Share
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 18
第12回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, June 29
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Group Accounting Issues for Multinational Companies
IFRS 3: Business Combinations, IFRS 10: Consolidated Financial Statements, IFRS 11: Joint Arrangements, IFRS 12: Disclosure of Interests in Other Entities
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 25, 26, 27
第13回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, July 2
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International Accounting for Foreign Currency Transactions and Translations 
IAS 21: The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates
Reading outside class: Alexander et al.: Chapter 28
第14回:
【Core Weeks】Monday, July 6
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International taxation and transfer pricing
Diversity of National Tax Systems, Taxation of Foreign-Source Income and Double Taxation, Tax-Planning Dimensions, International Transfer Pricing: Complicating Variables, Transfer Pricing Methodology, Transfer Pricing Practices.
Reading outside class: Choi and Meek: Chapter 12
第15回:
【Core Weeks】Thursday, July 9
"Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
International financial statements analysis 
Challenges and Opportunities in Cross-Border Analysis, Business Analysis Framework, International Business Strategy Analysis, Accounting Analysis, International Financial Analysis, International Prospective Analysis, Financial Statement Analysis and Auditing.
Reading outside class: Choi and Meek: Chapter 9
第16回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class (On-demand format)" /《90minutes》
Solution of case studies to be provided during in-person classes, selected exercises and problems from the text book.

学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Course Evaluation Form distributed in class.

Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
